info.name: E-Credentialing CVO Platform — Public REST API v1 (v1.10.0)
info.description:
The v1 surface is intentionally read-only. Every request requires
a Bearer API key with the appropriate scope; every request is
audited; PHI fields (SSN, DOB, DEA, personal address, notes) are
NEVER returned regardless of scope.

For a no-auth playground using synthetic data, see
`/api/sandbox/v1/*` (documented at `/sandbox`).

Rate limiting (since v1.2.0): every successful response carries
`X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and
`X-RateLimit-Reset` headers (Unix-seconds). When you exceed the
per-key budget the API returns a `429` with the `RateLimitProblem`
body and a `Retry-After` header.

Request correlation (since v1.3.0): every request and response —
success or error — carries an `X-Request-Id` header. Send your
own (`^[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{8,128}$`) and we'll honour it; otherwise
we generate one server-side. Paste it into a support ticket and
we can find your call in O(1). See `docs/api/versioning.md`
for the full contract and deprecation policy.

Key introspection (since v1.4.0): `GET /api/v1/me` returns the
current API key's name, granted scopes, lifecycle timestamps,
and current rate-limit budget. Pairs with `/health` to make
"is my key configured correctly?" a one-call answer.

Pagination links (since v1.5.0): every paginated list response
carries an RFC 8288 `Link` header listing `first`, `prev`,
`next`, and `last` page URLs. Clients can navigate the result
set without parsing the JSON envelope.

Conditional GETs (since v1.6.0): every read endpoint emits a
weak `ETag` response header derived from a stable hash of the
cacheable subset of the response body. Send the value back as
`If-None-Match: "<etag>"` on the next poll; if nothing has
changed the API replies `304 Not Modified` with an empty body
(zero JSON bytes). Saves bandwidth, DB round-trips, and your
rate-limit budget. The 304 still carries `X-Request-Id` and
the rate-limit headers.

Deprecation contract (since v1.7.0): an operation that has
entered its deprecation window emits the standard advisory
headers — `Deprecation` (RFC 9745) carrying the `@unix-seconds`
structured-fields integer when deprecation took effect,
`Sunset` (RFC 8594) carrying the IMF-fixdate when the operation
will start returning `410 Gone`, and `Link` entries with
`rel="deprecation"`, `rel="sunset"`, and (where one exists)
`rel="successor-version"` pointing at the upgrade guide and
replacement endpoint. **The headers are absent from operations
that are not on a deprecation path** — their presence is the
signal. The full lifecycle policy lives in
`docs/api/versioning.md` §4. The TypeScript SDK parses these
headers via `parseDeprecation(headers)` and emits a one-time
`console.warn` per process so customers find out before the
sunset wall-clock fires.

Problem Details for HTTP APIs (since v1.8.0): every error
response body is now an RFC 9457 Problem object — `type`
(stable URI per error code), `title` (short human-readable),
`status` (mirror of HTTP status), `detail` (occurrence-specific
message), and optional `instance` (the request path that
triggered the error). The legacy `{ error: { code, message,
...extras } }` envelope is preserved verbatim inside the same
body, so existing SDKs continue to read `body.error.code`
unchanged. The Content-Type defaults to
`application/problem+json` per RFC 9457 §3, except when the
caller's `Accept` header explicitly excludes it. Status codes
are unchanged. The TypeScript SDK exposes `parseProblem(body)`
and `V1ApiError.problem` for typed access; raw fetch callers
can read either shape.

Error catalog (since v1.10.0): the `type` URI inside every
Problem body resolves to a public, machine-readable catalog
entry. Browse the canonical HTML index at `/errors`, get the
raw JSON list at `GET /api/v1/errors`, or fetch a single
entry at `GET /api/v1/errors/{code}` (snake_case OR kebab-case
code accepted). Each catalog row carries a stable `code`,
`title`, `status`, one-sentence `summary`, full `description`,
concrete `remediation` guidance, the `sinceVersion` it was
introduced in, and an optional `retiredInVersion`. Retired
codes stay in the catalog forever so old `type` URIs in
SDKs in the wild keep resolving — see
`docs/dev/adr/0027-error-catalog.md` for the contract.

Server-side request validation (since v1.9.0): every paginated
list endpoint validates its query parameters with a strict Zod
schema before touching the database. Invalid input returns a
`400 Bad Request` Problem with `type` =
`…/errors/invalid-request` and a non-empty `errors[]` extension
array — each entry is `{ field, code, message }` where `field`
is the dot-joined query parameter path, `code` is a stable Zod
issue code (`too_small`, `too_big`, `invalid_enum_value`,
`invalid_type`, `invalid_string`, etc.), and `message` is the
English explanation. Multiple invalid parameters in one request
surface as multiple entries in `errors[]` — clients no longer
need to retry once per fix. The legacy `error` envelope and
`application/problem+json` content negotiation are unchanged.

Wave 8 (2026-04-18). See ADR 0020 and `docs/qa/per-pillar/pillar-j-api.md`.

Auto-generated from `docs/api/openapi-v1.yaml`.
Do not hand-edit — regenerate via `npm run postman:gen`.

variable.description: Base URL of your environment, no trailing slash.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on payer name.

request.description:
Returns the catalog entry for a single error `code`.
Both the snake_case form (e.g. `insufficient_scope`) and
the kebab-case URL suffix form (e.g.
`insufficient-scope` — the suffix of the `type` URI in
every Problem body) are accepted.

Returns `404 Not Found` for unknown codes; the response
is itself a Problem body with `type` =
`…/errors/not-found` and an extension member
`requestedCode` carrying the verbatim path parameter.

Authentication, cacheability, and versioning: same as
`GET /api/v1/errors`.

Returns the full v1 error catalog — every `code` the
platform can emit, with the same `title` / `status` the
wire contract uses, plus a `summary`, `description`,
`remediation`, and `sinceVersion`. The list is sorted by
`code` ascending and is identical for every caller.

Authentication: any active API key. Like `/health` and
`/me`, no specific scope is required — a brand-new,
scopeless key returns 200 here.

Cacheability: the catalog only changes when the platform
ships a new release, so the `ETag` is stable across
thousands of polls. Send the value back as
`If-None-Match` and the API replies `304 Not Modified`.

Versioning: shipped in v1.10.0 (SemVer minor — additive
only). See `docs/api/versioning.md` §3.10.

Verifies that the supplied API key is active and that the
environment is reachable. Returns the keyId so the caller can
confirm which key is in use without echoing the secret, the
API surface version, and a server-side timestamp for
clock-skew checks.

Authentication: ANY active API key. Unlike every other v1
operation, this endpoint does NOT require a specific scope
— a brand-new, scopeless key returns 200 here. This makes
it the natural first call when wiring up the SDK.

Versioning: shipped in v1.1.0 (SemVer minor — additive
only). See `docs/api/versioning.md`.

Returns the current API key's name, granted scopes,
creation/expiration/last-used timestamps, and the current
rate-limit budget snapshot. The canonical "what can my key
actually do?" check.

Authentication: ANY active API key. Like `/health`, no
specific scope is required - a brand-new, scopeless key
returns 200 here. This makes it useful for debugging "why
am I getting 403?" issues without a chicken-and-egg scope
requirement.

Privacy: never returns the bearer key, the key hash, or
any PHI. The `name` field is the human-readable label set
by the admin who created the key (e.g.
"Production prod-east"); it is safe to surface to the end
user as "you are authenticated as X".

Versioning: shipped in v1.4.0 (SemVer minor - additive
only). See `docs/api/versioning.md`.
Get one provider (full envelope, no PHI)

Streams a fully-formed Curriculum Vitae as `application/pdf`.
Requires the `providers:cv` scope. The CV includes only data
commonly published in CVs (name, NPI, provider type, education,
licensure, board certs, hospital privileges, work history,
CME log) — never SSN, DOB, DEA, or personal addresses.

Returns a paginated list of providers. Supports filtering by
`status` and `npi`. PHI is never included.
